# VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0002 - QUIC RFC 9001 TLS KeyUpdate Classifier Verification

## Scope

Prove that a real post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate message is classified as prohibited and reaches the existing KeyUpdateError close path, while NewSessionTicket remains separately supported and the managed key-phase install path stays distinct.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010

## Verification Method

Requirement-home tests plus focused execution and trace inspection.

## Preconditions

- The solution builds successfully.
- The RFC 9001 requirement-home tests can be discovered and executed locally.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests for the prohibited TLS KeyUpdate and connection-error clauses.
- Confirm the real TLS message-processing seam emits the prohibited-update state for a TLS KeyUpdate message and still emits post-handshake ticket publication for NewSessionTicket.
- Render the RFC 9001 SpecTrace markdown siblings and validate the JSON artifacts so the downstream trace reflects the new 0002 leaf slice.

## Expected Result

The requirement-home tests prove a real post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate is classified and closed through the existing KeyUpdateError path, NewSessionTicket remains separately supported, and the managed key-phase install path is still distinct.

## Evidence

- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010.cs

## Status

This verification slice proves the classifier seam plus the existing KeyUpdateError close path while keeping ticket publication and key-phase installation distinct.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-RFC9001
- ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0002
- WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0002

## Deferred Coverage

Broad TLS post-handshake behavior, beyond the KeyUpdate classifier and NewSessionTicket support already exercised here, remains out of scope.
